summary: First full MVP test pass. 20/20 backend pytest tests passing against public URL (health, auth, events CRUD, photos, tags, admin gating, cascade delete). All critical frontend flows verified on mobile viewport (375x667): home renders logo with yellow italic 'Salsa', hero 'The nights we danced.', 3 seeded event cards; tapping a card navigates to /event/{id}; share button copies link and shows share-toast; /login shows google-login-btn; /admin redirects to /login without token; with localStorage pcs_session_token set, /admin loads, create-event modal opens, event created and appears at top; admin per-event page shows upload-photos-btn; photo upload + lightbox + tag-yourself flow works end-to-end with '@pcs_test' chip visible.
action_items: Minor: review_request referenced testID 'event-submit-btn' for the create-event modal submit, but the actual component uses testID='submit-create-event'. Either is fine; document the correct testID in the playbook/spec so future tests are aligned.
Ngrok tunnel errors in /var/log/supervisor/expo.err.log are preview-infra noise (preview reaches backend via external URL). Safe to ignore.
Expo dependency version warnings: expo-clipboard, expo-file-system, expo-image-picker, expo-sharing are ahead of recommended Expo SDK versions. Not blocking but consider aligning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py logout: response.delete_cookie is invoked but when token arrived via Authorization header (no cookie) the client cookie clear is a no-op — acceptable since bearer clients manage their own storage.

server.py CORS is set to allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=True. Browsers reject credentialed requests with wildcard origin. Since current app is cookie-based for auth, this should be tightened to an explicit origin list for production. Not blocking since token is also sent via Authorization header.
server.py uses datetime in MongoDB for created_at/expires_at — motor serialises fine, but auth_me parses str fallback; consistent handling.
seed.py is idempotent for user and session (upsert) but skips re-seeding events if any exist — good for repeat runs.
ADMIN_EMAILS empty defaults to 'any Google user is admin' — intentional single-owner mode, but worth documenting as a security note for production.
seed_data_creation: Used existing seeded admin (test_session_pcs_admin_000) and 3 seeded events. Created TEST_UI_Event_NEW during UI testing + uploaded 1 base64 photo + 1 public tag; cleaned up via cascade DELETE /api/events/{id} at end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend public URL: https://rhythm-frames-3.preview.emergentagent.com (from EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). Admin bearer token: test_session_pcs_admin_000. For UI admin flow set localStorage.pcs_session_token to the same token. Run `python /app/backend/seed.py` to (re)create the admin session and 3 events. Pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_pcs_api.py covers all endpoints and cascades. Frontend uses testIDs including event-card-{i}, share-event-btn/share-ig-btn, share-toast, google-login-btn, create-event-btn, event-name-input, event-date-input, event-desc-input, submit-create-event (NOT event-submit-btn), admin-event-{id}, upload-photos-btn, photo-{i}, open-tag-sheet, tag-input, submit-tag.
rca_of_issue: No outstanding issues. End-to-end MVP (public browse → share → admin login via seeded session → create event → upload photo → public lightbox → self-tag) is fully functional on mobile web preview.
